debtSignals.title: 7 oversized source files
debtSignals.description: src/flask/app.py is 1,626 lines and among the most-edited files. Large files tend to accumulate mixed responsibilities.
debtSignals.methodology: Line count per source file in the most recent tree snapshot; threshold 450 lines (excludes tests, vendored and generated files).

debtSignals.title: Dependency concentration around hub modules
debtSignals.description: src/flask is connected to 6+ modules — changes there ripple widely.
debtSignals.methodology: Module in/out-degree on the latest import graph; flagged at ≥2.5× the mean degree (mean: 1.7).

debtSignals.title: 15 reverts in analyzed history
debtSignals.description: Frequent reverts suggest changes ship before their impact is understood.

refactorOpportunities.title: Reduce the blast radius of src/flask
evidence: src/flask is connected to 6+ modules — changes there ripple widely.
Detected via: Module in/out-degree on the latest import graph; flagged at ≥2.5× the mean degree (mean: 1.7).
refactorOpportunities.benefit: Fewer modules recompile, retest and break when the hub changes.
refactorOpportunities.risk: Splitting a hub that is genuinely cohesive adds indirection without payoff — verify the hub mixes concerns first.
refactorOpportunities.firstStep: List the distinct reasons other modules import src/flask; if there are ≥2 unrelated reasons, split along that line.

refactorOpportunities.title: Split src/flask/app.py
evidence: src/flask/app.py is 1,626 lines and among the most-edited files. Large files tend to accumulate mixed responsibilities.
Detected via: Line count per source file in the most recent tree snapshot; threshold 450 lines (excludes tests, vendored and generated files).
refactorOpportunities.benefit: Smaller units localize changes, shrink review surface and reduce merge conflicts.
refactorOpportunities.risk: Mechanical splitting without a responsibility boundary just spreads the tangle across files.
refactorOpportunities.firstStep: Group src/flask/app.py's top-level declarations by the data they touch; move the most independent group out first.

refactorOpportunities.title: Add tests to high-churn, low-test files
evidence: 6 frequently-changed source files never change together with a test file (src/flask/sansio/scaffold.py, src/flask/ctx.py, docs/conf.py).
Co-change with tests is a proxy: these files may still be covered indirectly.
refactorOpportunities.benefit: The files most likely to change next gain a safety net exactly where regressions are most likely.
refactorOpportunities.risk: Retro-fitted tests can freeze current behavior, bugs included — write them against intent, not implementation.
refactorOpportunities.firstStep: Start with src/flask/sansio/scaffold.py: cover its most recently changed code path first.

refactorOpportunities.title: Co-change across areas: src/flask/sansio/app.py ↔ flask/helpers.py
evidence: Changed together in 59 commits despite living in different architectural areas — a shared concern may be split across the boundary.
refactorOpportunities.benefit: Placing the shared concern in one home turns two-file edits into one-file edits.
refactorOpportunities.risk: Some cross-area co-change is legitimate (e.g. API + client); confirm the pairing is accidental before moving code.
refactorOpportunities.firstStep: Diff the last three commits that touched both files and name the concern they share.
